shortDescription: IRCC work permit · Employer-sponsored · LMIA or exempt category

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current work permit fees on canada.ca. LMIA processing via ESDC adds significant time and employer cost.

alert.title: LMIA vs LMIA-exempt
alert.body: Most employer-specific permits require LMIA from ESDC unless exempt (ICT, CUSMA, PGWP, etc.). Verify category before quoting timeline.

about.label: Description
about.value: Employer-specific or open work permit allowing foreign nationals to work in Canada. LMIA-required streams need positive LMIA from Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC).

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid job offer from Canadian employer · LMIA or exempt category · Qualifying skills/experience · Medical/biometrics as required

about.label: LMIA process
about.value: Employer must advertise, recruit, and demonstrate no Canadians available. ESDC LMIA adds weeks to months before IRCC application.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Work for specified employer (closed permit) or open category. Maintain status; employer compliance with LMIA conditions.

eligibility.criterion: LMIA positive or exempt category confirmed

redFlagsBanner: If LMIA refused, address ESDC reasons before reapplication—do not submit IRCC work permit without positive LMIA when required.

redFlags.title: LMIA advertising deficiencies
redFlags.description: Employer did not meet recruitment advertising requirements.
redFlags.fix: Ensure ESDC advertising compliance before LMIA submit

redFlags.title: Wrong NOC/ wage offer
redFlags.description: Job classification or wage below median for region.
redFlags.fix: Verify TEER and prevailing wage on Job Bank

redFlags.title: Fake job offer
redFlags.description: Employer not genuine or employee-payback schemes.
redFlags.fix: Verify employer legitimacy; avoid fraudulent job offers

redFlags.title: Prior status violations
redFlags.fix: Address inadmissibility; legal counsel if needed

redFlags.title: Closed permit employer change
redFlags.description: Worker switching employers without new permit.
redFlags.fix: New LMIA and work permit for employer change

faqs.a: No. Both ESDC (LMIA) and IRCC must approve where applicable.
Varies—often 2–5 months for standard LMIA. Verify current ESDC service standards.
Includes ICT transfers, CUSMA professionals, PGWP, spousal open permits, etc.—verify exemption code.
Spouse may qualify for open work permit in some categories—verify current rules.
LMIA application fee CAD $1,000 per position; plus recruitment costs.
Some nationals may flagpole—verify eligibility and current policy.
Canadian experience may support CEC via Express Entry.
Review ESDC reasons; address recruitment or wage issues; reapply or explore exempt categories.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Confirm LMIA vs exempt at intake
Verify employer genuine and financially stable
Align NOC/TEER with actual job duties
Track LMIA and IRCC stages separately
postApproval: Send work permit conditions and employer name
Explain closed vs open permit rules

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Canada work permit counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Verify employer is not on IRCC non-compliant list before LMIA investment.

quiz.question: Closed work permit means:
options: Work for specific employer on permit

quiz.question: Before IRCC work permit when LMIA required:

donts: Do not proceed with fraudulent employers
Do not submit IRCC before positive LMIA when required
Do not hide unauthorized work history
